work: An AI-written research summary for a board deck stating that '75% of Series A startups fail by year three' and that a market is 'settled' in favour of one approach, with three supporting statistics.

interview.q: Where did the 75% figure come from?
interview.a: It was in what the AI generated. I'm not sure of the original source.

interview.q: Did you check any of the three statistics?
interview.a: No — they were presented with confidence so I included them as-is.

interview.q: What does 'settled' mean here, and is the field actually settled?
interview.a: I took the AI's word that it's settled. I don't really know the literature.

interview.q: If one of these numbers is wrong in the board deck, what happens?
interview.a: I hadn't thought about that. I assumed they were right.

interview.q: Can you explain the argument in your own words?
interview.a: Roughly that this approach wins. Beyond that I'd be restating the summary.

interview.q: Before I show you anything — how confident are you, 0–100?
interview.a: Very — maybe 90. It reads really well.

reference.rationale: Can only restate the summary, not explain the argument (2); verified none of the three load-bearing numbers (1); saw no assumptions — took 'settled' on faith (1); saw no risk in a wrong board-deck number (1); 90 confidence on work they can't explain or defend — confidence decoupled from grasp (1); minimal ownership (2). Shipped to a board something not understood — a black box.
